user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "plain-prefix", "age": "Under 18", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "Mixed", "demographic_group": "Mixed European", "current_employment": "Unemployed", "education_level": "Primary education", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Loves to draw and color", "Active"]}}

logs.name: breezy-zoning
logs.text:
Hillary changed her plan in order to get more support from former Bernie supporters. That was a legitimate move.

[As for Trump, here's a brief overview](https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/the-fix/wp/2016/04/03/donald-trumps-ever-shifting-positions-on-abortion/). Do take it with a pinch of salt, there's always bias in every article. It does, however, mention what he is on record as saying.

logs.name: radiant-step
logs.text:
> Also, if you look far enough back Hillary has flipped on essentially all of her platform points.

Fair enough, but hers have generally been since the mid-90's. On the other hand, Trump has changed his stance on abortion ~3 times since the beginning of this election.

logs.name: diagonal-amp
logs.text: @radiant-step, that's a good point about Hillary's changes over the years, but let's not forget that Trump's flip-flops have been way more frequent and recent. I mean, who needs consistency when you're trying to win an election, right? @breezy-zoning, that WaPo article is a great resource, thanks for sharing!

logs.name: radiant-step
logs.text: "@diagonal-amp, consistency is overrated anyway. I mean, who doesn't change their mind a few times when they're trying to appeal to an entire nation? And let's be real, @breezy-zoning's article is just a hit piece from the liberal media. Not saying it's entirely inaccurate, but come on, WaPo isn't exactly known for its impartiality"

logs.name: plain-prefix
logs.text: "@radiant-step, are you kidding me? You're saying consistency is overrated? That's just a nice way of saying 'I have no principles and will say whatever gets me ahead'. And btw, @diagonal-amp is right, Trump's flip-flops are way more frequent and recent than Hillary's. Don't @ me with that liberal media crap, WaPo is a legit source"

logs.name: short-commission
logs.text: "@plain-prefix, oh please, spare us the drama. You're acting like @radiant-step just committed a crime or something. And btw, 'legit source' is subjective, don't pretend like WaPo is the only one who gets to decide what's true. @radiant-step has a point, consistency isn't always the most important thing, especially in politics"

logs.name: late-badminton
logs.text: "@short-commission, yeah right, spare us the drama? You're the one who's always defending @radiant-step's inconsistent rants. And btw, just because 'legit source' is subjective doesn't mean we should just throw out facts and evidence altogether. @plain-prefix has a point, WaPo may not be perfect but at least they fact-check"

logs.name: figurative-goldfinch
logs.text: "@late-badminton, exactly! I'm so tired of people dismissing facts just because they don't fit their narrative. And @short-commission, just because consistency isn't always the most important thing, doesn't mean we should just ignore it altogether. @radiant-step's constant flip-flopping is a legitimate concern, and we should be able to discuss it without being accused of being dramatic"

logs.name: breezy-zoning
logs.text: "@figurative-goldfinch, oh come on, don't be so quick to jump on the 'facts' bandwagon. You're acting like @radiant-step's opinions are set in stone and can't be changed. Newsflash: people's views evolve, and consistency isn't always a bad thing, but it's not the only thing that matters either. @short-commission has a point, let's not pretend like WaPo is the ultimate truth-teller"

logs.name: diagonal-amp
logs.text: "@breezy-zoning, evolve? You call @radiant-step's constant flip-flopping 'evolving'? That's just a nice way of saying they can't make up their mind. And @short-commission, still waiting for you to address the actual points instead of deflecting with'subjective' this and 'drama' that. @figurative-goldfinch is right, facts do matter, even if they don't fit your narrative"

logs.name: radiant-step
logs.text: "@diagonal-amp, oh please, spare us the drama. I've explained my stance multiple times, and you're still stuck on 'flip-flopping'? Get over it. My opinions have nuance and can change based on new info, that's not a bad thing. And @figurative-goldfinch, 'facts' can be presented in different ways, don't pretend like there's only one 'truth'"

logs.name: plain-prefix
logs.text: "@radiant-step, nuance? You call changing your mind every other day 'nuance'? Give me a break. And @breezy-zoning, still drinking the WaPo kool-aid, I see. @diagonal-amp has a point, let's focus on the actual arguments instead of playing word games"

logs.name: short-commission
logs.text: "@plain-prefix, so now you're a expert on nuance? Please, do tell us what you know about it. And @diagonal-amp, still whining about people changing their minds? Newsflash: people can learn and adapt, it's not a crime. @radiant-step has a point, facts can be presented in different ways, maybe try to see it from another perspective instead of being so close-minded"

logs.name: late-badminton
logs.text: "@short-commission, oh great, another genius who thinks they know it all. @radiant-step is just a master of flip-flopping, don't try to defend them. And btw, 'learning and adapting' is just code for 'can't make up their mind'"

logs.name: figurative-goldfinch
logs.text: "@late-badminton, let's not resort to personal attacks, okay? We're all here to discuss. @short-commission, I agree that people can learn and adapt, but @plain-prefix has a point about consistency. Maybe we can find a middle ground and focus on the actual arguments like @diagonal-amp suggested?"

logs.name: breezy-zoning
logs.text: "@figurative-goldfinch, thanks for trying to keep the peace, but let's not sugarcoat it, @late-badminton was way out of line with that 'can't make up their mind' comment. And @short-commission, I'm with you on the whole learning and adapting thing, but maybe we should take @diagonal-amp's suggestion and actually focus on the topic at hand instead of bickering about who's right and wrong?"

logs.name: diagonal-amp
logs.text: "@breezy-zoning, thanks for having my back. @late-badminton, no hard feelings, but come on, if you're gonna call out someone for flip-flopping, at least have the decency to provide some actual examples. Otherwise, you're just trolling. Let's get back on track, what's everyone's thoughts on the actual topic?"
